message:
fix(dlfcn): reject non-NUL-terminated names in strict mode (dlopen, dlsym)

REVIEW round 4 (same defense class as bd-z4k96 / iconv_open):
dlopen and dlsym both bounded-scanned the user pointer for length
but only acted on the result if `mode.heals_enabled()` was true —
i.e. ONLY in hardened mode. In strict mode (the more demanding
guarantee level) the check fell through and the subsequent
CStr::from_ptr(filename) / host_dlopen(filename) / host_dlsym(symbol)
calls walked arbitrary process memory.

The strict-mode suppression of the safety check is backwards: strict
mode must enforce at least the hardened safety floor, never less.

Fix: drop the `&& mode.heals_enabled()` from both rejection sites so
unterminated inputs return ERR_NOT_FOUND / ERR_SYMBOL_NOT_FOUND in
every mode. Replace the redundant `CStr::from_ptr(filename).to_bytes()`
in dlopen with the bounded `name_bytes` slice already captured from
the scan.

Reachability: dlopen / dlsym are called by every plugin-loading
program; an attacker-supplied or corrupted filename / symbol pointer
becomes an info-leak or segfault in strict mode.

Full abi suite: 2976/2976 pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
